{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR / Admin Manager",
  "description": "Velvet Casa Construction is a luxury construction company dedicated to crafting exquisite, high-quality living spaces that embody luxury, comfort and sophistication. With a team of experienced professionals, we deliver unparalled customer experiences and build lifelong clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR / Admin Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Job Responsibilities


	Oversee daily HR and administrative operations of the company
	Manage recruitment, onboarding, and staff welfare
	Develop and implement HR policies and procedures
	Maintain employee records and ensure compliance with labor regulations
	Coordinate staff training, performance management, and appraisals
	Supervise office administration and ensure smooth workplace operations
	Handle disciplinary matters and conflict resolution professionally
	Support management in strategic planning and organizational development.


Requirements


	Good educational qualification in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related fields
	Minimum of 10 years of proven work experience in HR/Admin management
	Strong communication, organizational, and leadership skills
	Ability to manage administrative operations effectively
	Professional certifications will be an added advantage.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: info@velvetcasaltd.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

HR / Admin Manager Job at Velvet Casa Construction




        
    

        
        Velvet Casa Construction is a luxury construction company dedicated to crafting exquisite, high-quality living spaces that embody luxury, comfort and sophistication. With a team of experienced professionals, we deliver unparalled customer experiences and build lifelong clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR / Admin Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Job Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Oversee daily HR and administrative operations of the company
	Manage recruitment, onboarding, and staff welfare
	Develop and implement HR policies and procedures
	Maintain employee records and ensure compliance with labor regulations
	Coordinate staff training, performance management, and appraisals
	Supervise office administration and ensure smooth workplace operations
	Handle disciplinary matters and conflict resolution professionally
	Support management in strategic planning and organizational development.


Requirements


	Good educational qualification in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related fields
	Minimum of 10 years of proven work experience in HR/Admin management
	Strong communication, organizational, and leadership skills
	Ability to manage administrative operations effectively
	Professional certifications will be an added advantage.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Residence Marketer",
  "description": "Casalavoro Limited is a fast-growing company with diverse interests in Real Estate, Hospitality, Asset Management, and Agricultural Commodity Trading. We&#39;re committed to excellence and innovation, delivering top-tier services that set new industry standards. Our expertise and passion drive growth, reliability, and exceptional client experiences.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Residence Marketer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)

Key Responsibilities


	Implement innovative sales and marketing strategies to consistently attract short-stay and long-stay guests.
	Source and convert leads through corporate partnerships, relocation agents, events, social media, referrals, and other relevant channels.
	Collaborate with the relationship officers to build and maintain relationships with past, existing, and prospective clients to maximize repeat business and referrals.
	Collaborate with the media team to promote rooms, amenities, offers, and packages across digital platforms.
	Promote seasonal offers, corporate packages, and loyalty programs through targeted campaigns.
	Consistently meet or exceed monthly booking and revenue targets.
	Stay up to date on competitor offerings and hospitality trends to keep CasaLavoro competitive.
	Prepare weekly and monthly reports on sales activities, conversion rates, and marketing effectiveness.
	Work closely with operations, customer service, and digital marketing teams to ensure a consistent and premium guest experience.


Requirements


	BSc / HND in Marketing, Business, Hospitality Management, or a related field with 2&ndash;3 years of proven experience in marketing or sales within the hospitality or hotel industry.
	Demonstrated ability to bring in guests/bookings.
	Strong communication, interpersonal, and negotiation skills.
	Self-driven with a proactive mindset and target-oriented approach.
	Familiarity with Abuja&rsquo;s hospitality and short-let market is a strong advantage.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: hr@casalavoro.net using &quot;Residence Marketer&quot; as the subject of ther email.",

Residence Marketer Job at Casalavoro Limited




        
    

        
        Casalavoro Limited is a fast-growing company with diverse interests in Real Estate, Hospitality, Asset Management, and Agricultural Commodity Trading. We're committed to excellence and innovation, delivering top-tier services that set new industry standards. Our expertise and passion drive growth, reliability, and exceptional client experiences.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Residence Marketer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Implement innovative sales and marketing strategies to consistently attract short-stay and long-stay guests.
	Source and convert leads through corporate partnerships, relocation agents, events, social media, referrals, and other relevant channels.
	Collaborate with the relationship officers to build and maintain relationships with past, existing, and prospective clients to maximize repeat business and referrals.
	Collaborate with the media team to promote rooms, amenities, offers, and packages across digital platforms.
	Promote seasonal offers, corporate packages, and loyalty programs through targeted campaigns.
	Consistently meet or exceed monthly booking and revenue targets.
	Stay up to date on competitor offerings and hospitality trends to keep CasaLavoro competitive.
	Prepare weekly and monthly reports on sales activities, conversion rates, and marketing effectiveness.
	Work closely with operations, customer service, and digital marketing teams to ensure a consistent and premium guest experience.


Requirements


	BSc / HND in Marketing, Business, Hospitality Management, or a related field with 2–3 years of proven experience in marketing or sales within the hospitality or hotel industry.
	Demonstrated ability to bring in guests/bookings.
	Strong communication, interpersonal, and negotiation skills.
	Self-driven with a proactive mindset and target-oriented approach.
	Familiarity with Abuja’s hospitality and short-let market is a strong advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using "Residence Marketer" as the subject of ther email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Utility Manager",
  "description": "Grand Oak Limited is part of a team committed to working together across companies. Only by sharing ideas, technologies and talents can the Group achieve and sustain profitable growth Keeping Commitment. We believe that following through on promises is the best way to maintain credibility with colleagues, employees and customers Continuous Improvement.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Utility Manager

Job Location: Sango Ota, Ogun

Key Responsibilities


	Supervise daily operations of all utility systems including boilers, compressors, chillers, generators, water treatment plants, and HVAC systems.
	Ensure continuous and efficient supply of utilities to support production activities.
	Monitor equipment performance and implement preventive and corrective maintenance schedules.
	Lead and coordinate a team of technicians and operators.
	Troubleshoot utility-related issues and implement timely solutions to minimize downtime.
	Maintain accurate records of utility consumption, maintenance activities, and system performance.
	Ensure compliance with safety regulations, environmental standards, and company policies.
	Support energy efficiency initiatives and cost reduction strategies.
	Liaise with external vendors and contractors for specialized maintenance or repairs.
	Participate in audits and inspections as required.


Requirements


	Educational Qualification: HND in Mechanical Engineering or any related Engineering discipline.
	Experience: Minimum of 3 years of experience in utility operations within an FMCG or manufacturing environment.
	Strong knowledge of industrial utility systems (steam, compressed air, water systems, refrigeration, power generation).
	Familiarity with maintenance planning and execution.
	Good leadership and team management skills.
	Basic knowledge of health, safety, and environmental practices.
	Strong problem-solving and decision-making abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and maintenance management systems is an advantage.

Utility Manager Job at Grand Oak Limited




        
    

        
        Grand Oak Limited is part of a team committed to working together across companies. Only by sharing ideas, technologies and talents can the Group achieve and sustain profitable growth Keeping Commitment. We believe that following through on promises is the best way to maintain credibility with colleagues, employees and customers Continuous Improvement.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Utility Manager

Job Location: Sango Ota, Ogun

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Supervise daily operations of all utility systems including boilers, compressors, chillers, generators, water treatment plants, and HVAC systems.
	Ensure continuous and efficient supply of utilities to support production activities.
	Monitor equipment performance and implement preventive and corrective maintenance schedules.
	Lead and coordinate a team of technicians and operators.
	Troubleshoot utility-related issues and implement timely solutions to minimize downtime.
	Maintain accurate records of utility consumption, maintenance activities, and system performance.
	Ensure compliance with safety regulations, environmental standards, and company policies.
	Support energy efficiency initiatives and cost reduction strategies.
	Liaise with external vendors and contractors for specialized maintenance or repairs.
	Participate in audits and inspections as required.


Requirements


	Educational Qualification: HND in Mechanical Engineering or any related Engineering discipline.
	Experience: Minimum of 3 years of experience in utility operations within an FMCG or manufacturing environment.
	Strong knowledge of industrial utility systems (steam, compressed air, water systems, refrigeration, power generation).
	Familiarity with maintenance planning and execution.
	Good leadership and team management skills.
	Basic knowledge of health, safety, and environmental practices.
	Strong problem-solving and decision-making abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and maintenance management systems is an advantage.

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Store Assistant",
  "description": "Grand Oak Limited (GOL) is the largest Manufacturer of SpiritsandWines, we believe our spirits should have character; an authenticity derived from where they are distilled, bottled and the philosophies of those who distil them.

There are many attributes that describe us resolute, self-sufficient, tough, hard-working, enduring, emotional, passionate, philosophical and engaging.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Store Assistant

Job Location: Sango Ota, Ogun

Job Description


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and physically fit Warehouse Store Assistant to support inventory and warehouse operations in a fast-paced FMCG environment.
	The role involves receiving, storing, picking, and dispatching goods while maintaining accurate stock records and ensuring compliance with safety standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive and inspect incoming goods for accuracy and quality
	Unload, sort, label, and store products in designated locations
	Pick, pack, and prepare orders for dispatch according to company standards
	Maintain accurate inventory records (manual or system-based)
	Conduct routine stock counts and assist in periodic stocktaking
	Monitor stock levels and report damages, shortages, or discrepancies
	Ensure proper stock rotation (FIFO/FEFO) to prevent expiry or wastage
	Keep the warehouse clean, organized, and hazard-free
	Operate basic warehouse equipment (e.g., pallet jacks, scanners)
	Follow all health, safety, and company procedures at all times


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess an OND in Business Administration, Logistics, Supply Chain Management, or Accounting with 1-2 years of experience.
	Basic understanding of inventory management and stock control
	Physically fit and able to lift heavy items
	Attention to detail and accuracy
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
	Basic literacy and numeracy skills


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Inventory and stock management
	Time management and efficiency
	Teamwork and communication
	Reliability and integrity
	Problem-solving and attention to detail


Working Conditions:


	Warehouse environment (may involve heat, dust, or cold storage areas)
	Prolonged standing, lifting, and manual handling
	Shift work, including weekends or overtime as required

Store Assistant Job at Grand Oak Limited




        
    

        
        Grand Oak Limited (GOL) is the largest Manufacturer of SpiritsandWines, we believe our spirits should have character; an authenticity derived from where they are distilled, bottled and the philosophies of those who distil them.

There are many attributes that describe us resolute, self-sufficient, tough, hard-working, enduring, emotional, passionate, philosophical and engaging.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Store Assistant

Job Location: Sango Ota, Ogun

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a detail-oriented and physically fit Warehouse Store Assistant to support inventory and warehouse operations in a fast-paced FMCG environment.
	The role involves receiving, storing, picking, and dispatching goods while maintaining accurate stock records and ensuring compliance with safety standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive and inspect incoming goods for accuracy and quality
	Unload, sort, label, and store products in designated locations
	Pick, pack, and prepare orders for dispatch according to company standards
	Maintain accurate inventory records (manual or system-based)
	Conduct routine stock counts and assist in periodic stocktaking
	Monitor stock levels and report damages, shortages, or discrepancies
	Ensure proper stock rotation (FIFO/FEFO) to prevent expiry or wastage
	Keep the warehouse clean, organized, and hazard-free
	Operate basic warehouse equipment (e.g., pallet jacks, scanners)
	Follow all health, safety, and company procedures at all times


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess an OND in Business Administration, Logistics, Supply Chain Management, or Accounting with 1-2 years of experience.
	Basic understanding of inventory management and stock control
	Physically fit and able to lift heavy items
	Attention to detail and accuracy
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
	Basic literacy and numeracy skills


Skills & Competencies:


	Inventory and stock management
	Time management and efficiency
	Teamwork and communication
	Reliability and integrity
	Problem-solving and attention to detail


Working Conditions:


	Warehouse environment (may involve heat, dust, or cold storage areas)
	Prolonged standing, lifting, and manual handling
	Shift work, including weekends or overtime as required


Note: Only OND candidates can apply.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Agricultural Operations Manager",
  "description": "Nine Stars Limited is a dynamic and innovative agricultural and real estate company committed to transforming Nigeria&rsquo;s market landscape. Based in Ogere-Remo,Ogun State, our company aims to provide unparalleled agricultural and real estate solutions, offering a wide range of services to meet the diverse needs of individuals and businesses alike.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Agricultural Operations Manager

Job Location: Ogere-Remo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Overview


	The company is developing a large-scale industrial new city project in Nigeria, currently in the construction and development stage. A large amount of land requires grass cutting, bush clearing, and tree removal, followed by long-term maintenance. In the future, some undeveloped land will be used for agricultural planting and operations.
	We are recruiting an experienced Agricultural Manager who can work hands-on in the field. This person will be responsible for managing land-clearing teams and agricultural planting operations.


Key Responsibilities
Overall Agricultural Planting Planning and Execution (Primary Responsibility):


	Develop annual/quarterly planting plans (crop selection, crop rotation plans)
	Manage the full planting process, including:
	Land preparation
	Sowing / seedling cultivation
	Fertilization and irrigation
	Pest and disease control
	Growth monitoring
	Harvesting and yield management
	Optimize crop output and agricultural profitability
	Establish standardized planting SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures)


Agricultural Production System and On-site Management:


	Establish farmland production management systems and operating standards
	Organize agricultural production teams to execute planting plans
	Monitor crop growth quality and yield performance
	Promote demonstration plots / trial fields
	Control production costs and improve input-output ratio


Agricultural Labor Team Management:


	Manage field labor teams (farmers, casual workers, seasonal workers)
	Responsible for recruitment, grouping, scheduling, and attendance tracking
	Establish piece-rate or performance incentive systems
	Manage foreman system
	Resolve labor disputes, conflicts, and discipline issues


Farmland Basic Maintenance:


	Responsible for basic farmland maintenance (weed control, boundary clearing, etc.)
	Ensure stable farming environment in planting areas
	Coordinate the use of basic tools and equipment (brush cutters, farming tools, etc.)


Agricultural Inputs and Cost Control:


	Manage seeds, fertilizers, pesticides, and other agricultural inputs
	Establish inventory and usage tracking system
	Control production cost and reduce waste
	Conduct basic review and planning for procurement


Government and Community Coordination:


	Maintain relationships with local communities and coordinate agriculture-related communication
	Support liaison with relevant agricultural government departments
	Assist in resolving basic coordination issues related to land/farmers


Data Recording and Reporting:


	Prepare agricultural production plans and execution reports
	Submit regular yield, cost, and problem analysis reports
	Record crop growth data and production results
	Support management decision-making and analysis


Basic Background Requirements


	Nigerian local candidate
	Recommended age: 40&ndash;50 years old
	(too young may lack authority; too old may lack physical strength/execution ability)
	Education: not strict requirement
	Major in agriculture / agronomy / land management / environmental management / forestry is preferred
	Language:
	
		Working English communication (must)
		Plus one major local language (Yoruba / Hausa / Igbo)
		Most Critical Experience Requirements (Mandatory)
	
	
	Real large-scale farmland / farm management experience
	Minimum 5 years of agricultural or farm operations experience
	Experience managing 50&ndash;200 seasonal workers/farm laborers is ideal
	Practical planting experience in at least one of the following crops:
	maize, cassava, vegetables, rice, banana, palm, etc.
	Experience in land clearing, land development, or forestry/estate management
	Skilled in grass cutting, tree cutting, bush clearing, drainage trench digging
	Familiar with common Nigerian tools such as:
	cutlass, brush cutter, chainsaw, etc.
	Experience in plantation / estate / large land clearing is a strong advantage
	Must Be Able to Manage People and Deliver Execution


The biggest challenge of this role is that the farming/labor workforce is scattered, undisciplined, often lazy, inefficient, and may cause conflicts.
Therefore, the candidate must have:


	Strong leadership and foreman management ability
	Scheduling, piece-rate payment design, and attendance control ability
	Experience handling injuries, conflicts, theft, etc.
	Ability to establish rules and systems (performance management, rewards/punishments, daily reports, weekly plans)
	Personality Traits (Very Important)
	Strong execution ability, willing to work in the field and supervise on site
	Not afraid of dirt, sun, or conflicts with workers
	Used to working closely with frontline laborers, not an office-based &ldquo;academic type&rdquo;
	Principle-driven and authoritative, but not violent/aggressive
	Avoid the Following Candidate Types
	Pure academic/research background (university lecturer, researcher) without team management experience
	NGO agricultural officers who only write reports, PPTs, or project proposals
	Small farm owners (only 5&ndash;10 acres)
	People overly dependent on connections and kickbacks (common in Nigeria)


Competency Model:
Land Clearing and Maintenance Capability:


	Able to develop clearing plans: which plots first, manpower required, days needed
	Able to estimate tool requirements, fuel usage, blade replacement, brush cutter maintenance
	Understand safety management (especially chainsaws, brush cutters, and controlled burning risks)


Labor Organization Capability:


	Recruit and manage foremen
	Design piece-rate wage system (per acre / per meter / per truckload, etc.)
	Attendance, discipline, and conflict management
	Control theft, tool loss, and material waste


Agricultural Planting Technical Capability:


	Crop and variety selection
	Basic agronomy: sowing, seedling raising, fertilization, irrigation, pest/disease control
	Harvesting and storage
	Able to run small-scale demonstration plots


Government / Community Coordination Capability:


	Able to communicate with village leaders, land communities, and farmer associations
	Understand local community &ldquo;sensitive issues.&rdquo;
	Prevent disputes caused by tree cutting/land clearing


Basic Administration and Reporting Capability:


	Able to write simple English reports
	Able to prepare weekly work plans and cost budgets
	Able to use mobile phone photos to record field progress
	Able to report and coordinate with the Chinese management team


Job Requirements


	Minimum 5 years of agricultural/farm/plantation operations management experience
	Experience in large-scale land clearing, land development, or plantation management preferred
	Experience managing large labor teams (50+ people preferred)
	Able to live on site and adapt to high-intensity outdoor field work
	English + local language
	Strong responsibility, execution ability, and ability to independently solve on-site issues.


Salary
N150,000 - N300,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter to: ninestars.recruitment@yahoo.com using &ldquo;Agricultural Operations Manager&rdquo; as the subject of the email.",

Agricultural Operations Manager Job at Nine Stars Limited




        
    

        
        Nine Stars Limited is a dynamic and innovative agricultural and real estate company committed to transforming Nigeria’s market landscape. Based in Ogere-Remo,Ogun State, our company aims to provide unparalleled agricultural and real estate solutions, offering a wide range of services to meet the diverse needs of individuals and businesses alike.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Agricultural Operations Manager

Job Location: Ogere-Remo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The company is developing a large-scale industrial new city project in Nigeria, currently in the construction and development stage. A large amount of land requires grass cutting, bush clearing, and tree removal, followed by long-term maintenance. In the future, some undeveloped land will be used for agricultural planting and operations.
	We are recruiting an experienced Agricultural Manager who can work hands-on in the field. This person will be responsible for managing land-clearing teams and agricultural planting operations.


Key Responsibilities
Overall Agricultural Planting Planning and Execution (Primary Responsibility):


	Develop annual/quarterly planting plans (crop selection, crop rotation plans)
	Manage the full planting process, including:
	Land preparation
	Sowing / seedling cultivation
	Fertilization and irrigation
	Pest and disease control
	Growth monitoring
	Harvesting and yield management
	Optimize crop output and agricultural profitability
	Establish standardized planting SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures)


Agricultural Production System and On-site Management:


	Establish farmland production management systems and operating standards
	Organize agricultural production teams to execute planting plans
	Monitor crop growth quality and yield performance
	Promote demonstration plots / trial fields
	Control production costs and improve input-output ratio


Agricultural Labor Team Management:


	Manage field labor teams (farmers, casual workers, seasonal workers)
	Responsible for recruitment, grouping, scheduling, and attendance tracking
	Establish piece-rate or performance incentive systems
	Manage foreman system
	Resolve labor disputes, conflicts, and discipline issues


Farmland Basic Maintenance:


	Responsible for basic farmland maintenance (weed control, boundary clearing, etc.)
	Ensure stable farming environment in planting areas
	Coordinate the use of basic tools and equipment (brush cutters, farming tools, etc.)


Agricultural Inputs and Cost Control:


	Manage seeds, fertilizers, pesticides, and other agricultural inputs
	Establish inventory and usage tracking system
	Control production cost and reduce waste
	Conduct basic review and planning for procurement


Government and Community Coordination:


	Maintain relationships with local communities and coordinate agriculture-related communication
	Support liaison with relevant agricultural government departments
	Assist in resolving basic coordination issues related to land/farmers


Data Recording and Reporting:


	Prepare agricultural production plans and execution reports
	Submit regular yield, cost, and problem analysis reports
	Record crop growth data and production results
	Support management decision-making and analysis


Basic Background Requirements


	Nigerian local candidate
	Recommended age: 40–50 years old
	(too young may lack authority; too old may lack physical strength/execution ability)
	Education: not strict requirement
	Major in agriculture / agronomy / land management / environmental management / forestry is preferred
	Language:
	
		Working English communication (must)
		Plus one major local language (Yoruba / Hausa / Igbo)
		Most Critical Experience Requirements (Mandatory)
	
	
	Real large-scale farmland / farm management experience
	Minimum 5 years of agricultural or farm operations experience
	Experience managing 50–200 seasonal workers/farm laborers is ideal
	Practical planting experience in at least one of the following crops:
	maize, cassava, vegetables, rice, banana, palm, etc.
	Experience in land clearing, land development, or forestry/estate management
	Skilled in grass cutting, tree cutting, bush clearing, drainage trench digging
	Familiar with common Nigerian tools such as:
	cutlass, brush cutter, chainsaw, etc.
	Experience in plantation / estate / large land clearing is a strong advantage
	Must Be Able to Manage People and Deliver Execution


The biggest challenge of this role is that the farming/labor workforce is scattered, undisciplined, often lazy, inefficient, and may cause conflicts.
Therefore, the candidate must have:


	Strong leadership and foreman management ability
	Scheduling, piece-rate payment design, and attendance control ability
	Experience handling injuries, conflicts, theft, etc.
	Ability to establish rules and systems (performance management, rewards/punishments, daily reports, weekly plans)
	Personality Traits (Very Important)
	Strong execution ability, willing to work in the field and supervise on site
	Not afraid of dirt, sun, or conflicts with workers
	Used to working closely with frontline laborers, not an office-based “academic type”
	Principle-driven and authoritative, but not violent/aggressive
	Avoid the Following Candidate Types
	Pure academic/research background (university lecturer, researcher) without team management experience
	NGO agricultural officers who only write reports, PPTs, or project proposals
	Small farm owners (only 5–10 acres)
	People overly dependent on connections and kickbacks (common in Nigeria)


Competency Model:
Land Clearing and Maintenance Capability:


	Able to develop clearing plans: which plots first, manpower required, days needed
	Able to estimate tool requirements, fuel usage, blade replacement, brush cutter maintenance
	Understand safety management (especially chainsaws, brush cutters, and controlled burning risks)


Labor Organization Capability:


	Recruit and manage foremen
	Design piece-rate wage system (per acre / per meter / per truckload, etc.)
	Attendance, discipline, and conflict management
	Control theft, tool loss, and material waste


Agricultural Planting Technical Capability:


	Crop and variety selection
	Basic agronomy: sowing, seedling raising, fertilization, irrigation, pest/disease control
	Harvesting and storage
	Able to run small-scale demonstration plots


Government / Community Coordination Capability:


	Able to communicate with village leaders, land communities, and farmer associations
	Understand local community “sensitive issues.”
	Prevent disputes caused by tree cutting/land clearing


Basic Administration and Reporting Capability:


	Able to write simple English reports
	Able to prepare weekly work plans and cost budgets
	Able to use mobile phone photos to record field progress
	Able to report and coordinate with the Chinese management team


Job Requirements


	Minimum 5 years of agricultural/farm/plantation operations management experience
	Experience in large-scale land clearing, land development, or plantation management preferred
	Experience managing large labor teams (50+ people preferred)
	Able to live on site and adapt to high-intensity outdoor field work
	English + local language
	Strong responsibility, execution ability, and ability to independently solve on-site issues.


Salary
N150,000 - N300,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter to: [email protected] using “Agricultural Operations Manager” as the subject of the email.
